sourceList.title: Legacy NFS Source: Alix Capet - birth-name: Alix Capet
Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - HEDWIGE [Avoie] de France, daughter of ROBERT II "le Pieux" King of France & his third wife Constance d'Arles [Provence] ([1003]-5 Jun after 1063).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France於1003年出生在法國布格尼艾福內維斯時，她的父親Robert II le Pieux roi de France31歲，母親Constance d'Arles31歲。她和Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1063年6月5日之後在她的家鄉，埋葬在法國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France于1003年出生在法国布格尼艾福內維斯时，她的父亲Robert II le Pieux roi de France31岁，母亲Constance d'Arles31岁。她和Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1063年6月5日之后在她的家乡，埋葬在法国。

nameMeanings.name: France
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from France (see 2 below).

French: habitational name given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris); or, in southern France, an ethnic name for someone from what is now the northern half of the country, where the langue d'oïl (Old French as distinct from Occitan) was spoken. Compare   Defrance   and   Lafrance  .
Czech (Franče): from a pet form of František, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Defrance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French (mainly northern): habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris). Compare France .

nameMeanings.name: Adelaide
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin (via French Adélaïde ), from adal ‘noble’ + heid ‘kind, sort’. It was borne in the 10th century by the wife of the Holy Roman Emperor Otto the Great. She became regent after his death and was revered as a saint. The given name increased in popularity in England during the 19th century, when it was borne by the wife of King William IV; she was the daughter of the ruler of the German duchy of Saxe-Meiningen. The Australian city of Adelaide was named in her honour.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

en.lifeSummary: When Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France was born in 1003, in Nevers, Nièvre, Burgundy, France, her father, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, was 31 and her mother, Constance d'Arles, was 31. She had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre. She died after 5 June 1063, in her hometown, and was buried in France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France est née en 1003, à Nevers, Nièvre, Bourgogne, France, son père, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Constance d'Arles, avait 31 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre. Elle est décédée après 5 juin 1063, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterrée à France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France 1003 in Nevers, Nièvre, Burgund, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert II le Pieux roi de France 31 und ihre Mutter Constance d'Arles 31. Sie hatte mit Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 5. Juni 1063 in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France è nata il nel 1003 nel Nevers, Nièvre, Bourgogne, Francia, sua padre, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Constance d'Arles, aveva 31 anni. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre. Morì dopo il 5 giugno 1063, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolta a Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of Franceが1003年にフランスボウルゴグンニevアネバーズで生まれた時，父Robert II le Pieux roi de Franceは31歳，母Constance d'Arlesは31歳であった。彼女はRenauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerreとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1063年6月5日以後に故郷で死去し，フランスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France(이)가 1003년 프랑스 부르고그니 니e브르 네버스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert II le Pieux roi de France(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Constance d'Arles(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1063년 6월 5일 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했으며 프랑스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France nasceu em 1003, em Nevers, Nièvre, Bourgogne, França, seu pai, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Constance d'Arles, tinha 31 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre. Ela faleceu após 5 de junho de 1063, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultada em França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France родилась в 1003 г. в местности нивирс, ниeвр, бургогн, Франция, её отцу по имени Robert II le Pieux roi de France было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Constance d'Arles, 31. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre. Она умерла после 5 июнь 1063 г. в её родном городе и была похоронена в местности Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adelaide Havoise de Auxerre of France nació en 1003, en Nevers, Nièvre, Bourgogne, Francia, su papá, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Constance d'Arles, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Renauld de Nevers I, Comte de Nevers ou Auxerre. Murió después de 5 de junio de 1063, en su pueblo, y fue sepultada en Francia.
